channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: France's foreign minister is in China to discuss Ukraine and trade issues
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot began a two-day visit to China on Thursday and held talks with his Chinese counterpart on Ukraine and longstanding trade disputes between China and Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Brazilian President Lula swipes at Trump as US imposes auto tariffs
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva swiped Thursday at U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to raise U.S. tariffs on a wide range of products, saying Trump should consider the potential harm to the American and global economies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Firefighters make progress on containing some Carolina wildfires, but others grow
item.description: Firefighters announced progress Wednesday night on containing two of the largest wildfires burning in the North Carolina mountains, but they warned that fire danger remained from dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pasadena reaches agreement to host Olympic soccer at Rose Bowl
item.description: The City of Pasadena has reached an agreement with the organizers of the 2028 Olympic and Paralympic Games to host Olympic soccer at Rose Bowl Stadium. Men's and women's semifinal and final matches will be held at the historic venue when Los Angeles hosts the games in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Pilot rescued with 2 girls from icy Alaska lake was not authorized to have passengers, official says
item.description: A pilot rescued with two young family members after they survived a night on the wing of an airplane partially submerged in an Alaska lake is a student pilot who was not authorized to fly with passengers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, ABC 4

item.title: Kansas measles cases double to 23 and new Ohio outbreak sickens 10
item.description: A measles outbreak in Kansas doubled in less than a week to 23 cases and has "a possible link” to outbreaks in Texas and New Mexico that have sickened more than 370, the state health department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NATO issues new statement on fate of 4 missing Fort Stewart soldiers
item.description: WIESBADEN, Germany (WSAV) — The NATO Acting spokesperson said that the Secretary General was referring to news articles and did not confirm the death of four U.S. Army 3rd Infantry Division soldiers reported missing in Lithuania Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Education Department restores IDR student loan applications
item.description: The applications were put on pause after a federal appeals court ruled the Biden administration’s Saving on Valuable Education (SAVE) income-drive repayment (IDR) plan was illegal.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kristi Noem visiting El Salvador to tour Terrorist Confinement Center
item.description: Noem's slated to meet with El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele, who the administration has touted as a great partner.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, NewsNation

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers who were killed when the road they were repairing buckled underneath them.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into a pair of socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to a court recently in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former NFL player LeShon Johnson charged in FBI's largest dog fighting bust, 190 animals seized: indictment
item.description: Former NFL running back LeShon Eugene Johnson has been indicted on 21 charges in a federal dog fighting bust that led to the recovery of 190 pit bulls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Duke football player confesses to killing ex-real estate agent friend in her upscale townhome: police
item.description: Former Duke University football player Brandon Braxton has allegedly confessed to killing his former high school friend and Charlotte, North Carolina, real estate agent Whitney Hurd.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina minimum wage could increase to $22 if bill is approved
item.description: North Carolina lawmakers will consider increasing minimum wage this session after two bills were filed proposing higher wages.
item.dc:creator: Judith Retana, NewsNation

item.title: Sewage sludge can find a second life on farm fields. Here's how it's made
item.description: What goes down your toilet can end up on farm fields across the United States. Biosolids, or sewage sludge, are the solid byproducts of the wastewater treatment process. Rich in nutrients, they can be used as fertilizer on agricultural fields or compost on lawns.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance to accompany wife to Greenland amid backlash
item.description: Vance’s announcement adds another layer of tension to an already fraught atmosphere surrounding his wife’s planned visit.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Iowa law banning school library books that depict sex acts on hold again after a new federal ruling
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa cannot, for now, continue to enforce part of its book ban law, a federal judge said Tuesday, giving major publishers that sued the state the second temporary reprieve they requested. The new decision from U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a trip to Nashville, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: GOP-led states push for control of school aid as Trump promises a smaller federal role in education
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Governors in several Republican-led states are pressing the Trump administration to cut strings attached to their federal education money, a goal conservatives have long dreamed of that now appears within reach as President Donald Trump moves to dismantle the Education Department.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexican cartel member stockpiled ammo on border
item.description: A Mexican-national member of the Cartel Del Noreste has been sentenced to five years in prison for illegally possessing ammunition on the South Texas border, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Illinois returns stolen land to Prairie Band Potawatomi Nation
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Prairie Land Potawatomi Nation has reclaimed land in Illinois that was promised to the tribe's leader 175 years ago but stolen by the federal government 20 years later. A law signed by Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Delta Air Lines unveils 2025 trading cards to celebrate 100 years
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: FBI launches Tesla vandalism task force
item.description: The FBI confirmed Monday it has established a task force to investigate the wave of vandalism and arson incidents targeting Tesla in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Do you eat your meal in less than 20 minutes? It might be time to slow down
item.description: Experts tend to focus on the kinds of foods you can eat to improve your health. But the speed at which you devour your dinner matters just as much.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
